title: Choc pot in 2 minutes – microwave

seo.title: Choc pot in 2 minutes - microwave - Marinda Kook

html:
I've just eaten the mug of chocolate pudding and I think I'm satisfied for 2 weeks. It's rich, it's moist and it's decadent and it's quick. It's ideal for guests who show up unexpectedly. Serve it with cream, ice cream or custard.

 

NEEDED:

  • 80g butter
  • 80 g chocolate (I used whole-nut, but you can use any chocolate)
  • 30g cake flour
  • 30g sugar
  • pinch of salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon vanilla essence
  • 1 egg
  • 2 blocks of chocolate

NOW:

  1. melt the butter and chocolate for 1 minute in a bowl in the microwave – stir it
  2. mix the flour, sugar, salt, egg and vanilla essence with a wire whisk
  3. mix the warm chocolate/butter mixture into your flour mixture
  4. spray two mugs with spray and cook and divide the mixture between the two mugs
  5. press another 2 blocks of chocolate into each mug – it must be covered under the batter
  6. microwave on high for 90 seconds – if it's not yet coming away from the sides, microwave for another 30 seconds

Be warned: this dessert is rich. It's deliciously moist and calls for more.

You can eat it in the mug or loosen the sides and turn it out onto a little plate.
